design_philosophy.core_loop: Select from roster of recruitable warriors with distinct weapons, navigate maze-like levels to reach exit or eliminate all enemies before time expires, rescue captive allies and escort them to safety
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with resource management—managing limited party members and deciding whether to pursue exit or complete elimination objective
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a squad of diverse soldiers through historical war simulations, rescuing POWs from a rogue supercomputer's deadly scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid objective system (reach exit OR destroy all enemies) creates moment-to-moment decision-making; roster-based character swapping emphasizes tactical flexibility over single-character mastery; heavy emphasis on cooperative multi-player strategy rather than solo performance

difficulty.lives_system: Party-based: game over when all controlled characters killed without any reaching exit; defeated characters become captives that can be rescued or lost
difficulty.continues_system: Three continues allow player to restart level with original three characters
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hard mode removes captive rescue mechanic but starts with all twenty characters available, creating alternate difficulty path with different strategic constraints

progression.upgrade_system: Character roster expansion through rescue and recruitment of captive warriors; each warrior possesses distinct weapon loadouts affecting tactical options
progression.unlock_mechanism: Captive characters must be reached and escorted to exit to join playable roster; Hard mode unlocks with all characters available from start
replayability_hooks: Dual-objective system (exit vs. elimination) creates different route planning per level
Twenty recruitable warriors with distinct weapons encourage replaying with different squad compositions
Genesis version adds Modern Epoch (10 additional levels) beyond arcade's 40 levels
Master System version includes 10 unique levels set in 'Gain Ground Main System' itself

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design—levels organized by historical periods of human history (multiple epochs)
Premise-driven worldbuilding via arcade flyer: supercomputer simulation in year 2348 to restore humanity's 'waning fighting spirit' becomes rogue threat
Environmental storytelling through epoch progression: Ancient, Medieval, Renaissance, Modern, Future periods establish temporal narrative arc

boss_design.philosophy: Capstone encounters marking end of each four-level round; bosses serve as progression gates and difficulty peaks

faction_indicators: Federated Government (creates and deploys Gain Ground simulation)
Three 'bravest warriors' dispatched as player party
danger_signals: Hostage situation (POWs held in simulation levels)
Artificial war training masking actual combat stress
environmental_storytelling: Historical epoch levels suggest simulation is teaching/testing warriors across time periods
Progression from Ancient through Future epochs implies systematic military training curriculum
Captive civilians in each level indicate collateral impact of simulation malfunction
